inline_comments.message: seems this is replacing the other operator, I believe you would simply add another statement? Or is this fixing code?

good question, in essence no change in this file, just formatting I leaked here.
the A1pActor use the same SdnrOperation class as SdnrActor.  It
does not inherit from SdnrActor (it was giving me some trouble when I tried it out).
The A1pOperation does inherit from SdnrOperation and all that code  from SdnrOperation
is pretty much reused.
So, here it just calls the 2-arguments super constructor from Bidirectional...  
As a note, at runtime both actors use the same Operations and message format but over different topics.   I couldn't figure out a better way than creating another actor to go through
different request/response topics.   Here is the a1p constructor for convenience comparing it:

public class A1pActor extends BidirectionalTopicActor<BidirectionalTopicActorParams>
..
    public A1pActor() {
        super(NAME, BidirectionalTopicActorParams.class);

        addOperator(new BidirectionalTopicOperator(NAME, SdnrOperation.NAME, this, SdnrOperation.SELECTOR_KEYS,
            A1pOperation::new));
    }
